description: The Halal Awareness Course aims to provide a deeper understanding of the potential of the Halal market, Halal management systems and their importance in daily life and business. This course aims to raise the awareness of individuals and organisations about the prospects of the Halal market, compliance with Halal principles and ensuring that the products and services offered meet Halal standards recognised by authorities such as JAKIM. venue: Online Webinar by Acad Systems KL, B3-1, Dataran Palma, Jalan Selaman 1, Ampang, Selangor, 68000, Malaysia
description:
Semi-Detailed Distillation Column Design with Sieve Tray Arrangement Using AutoCAD Plant 3D
 
Who Should Attend: Chemical and Biotechnology Engineering Students, Mechanical Engineering Students Interested in Process Systems, AutoCAD Plant 3D Beginners, CAD Users Transitioning to Plant Design, Entry-Level Process or Plant Engineers, Engineering Interns, Individuals Interested in Basic Distillation Design and Layout. 
Join us for a free online AutoCAD Plant 3D session where we will demonstrate how to model a semi-detailed distillation column through a clear and structured workflow. This session is suitable for engineering students, beginners, and anyone interested in process equipment modelling. You will learn how to set up your project, create the distillation column vessel, and arrange simplified sieve trays using AutoCAD Plant 3D tools. The session also includes a simple structural arrangement to help participants build a clean and organized model. 
In this session, we will cover: 

Basic modelling of a semi-detailed distillation column and simplified sieve tray arrangement
Creating and arranging internal tray structures inside the column

description: It is now well recognised that pain intensity does not correlate that well with the extent or seriousness of pathology, that wide variation exists in the response to identical noxious/nociceptive input and to the provision of identical therapies and that susceptibility to develop certain painful disorders is also variable. This means that we need to think much more broadly about pain than it simply being a consequence of presenting pathology and rather, consider ALL possible contributing factors in ANY pain state, regardless whether it is acute or persistent. Many of the reasons for this variability are understood and the aims of this course are to demonstrate: • the extraordinary complexity of pain and how to assess all aspects of this complexity, including baseline characteristics (modulators) that can influence pain outcomes, as well as the (often modifiable) factors that can mediate the relationship between an individual’s modulators and their outcome • how to break this complexity down to the components of relevance so as then to more readily be able to provide strategies that more comprehensively and effectively manage somebody’s pain

course_name: Safety and Health Officer (SHO) certification course
description: The Safety and Health Officer (SHO) certification course designed to enhance participants’ expertise in implementing and managing occupational safety and health (OSH) systems. The course covers historical development, incident prevention, OSH management systems, legislation, risk assessment, and emergency preparedness. It begins with the evolution of OSH, highlighting key figures and legislation that have shaped current practices. Participants will gain a deep understanding of OSH management principles, including policy development, risk assessment, and the use of personal protective equipment. The course also addresses compliance with Malaysian OSH laws, such as the Factories and Machinery Act 1967 and OSHA 1994, ensuring participants are well-versed in legal requirements. Practical skills covered include incident investigation, auditing, emergency response planning, and promoting OSH through training and communication. By the end of the course, participants will be equipped to develop and maintain effective OSH management systems, conduct risk assessments, ensure legal compliance, and foster a culture of safety within their organizations.

course_name: In-House Training Course on Management and Handling of Scheduled Wastes under EQSWR 2005
description: Conduct of training (EQSWR 2005, Regulation 15) Every waste generator shall ensure that all his employees involved in the identification, handling, labeling, transportation, storage and spillage or discharge response of scheduled wastes attend training programs. Airwastewater Management Sdn Bhd is pleased to offer a one day inhouse training course on the management and handling of scheduled wastes. The aim of the course is to assist industries to comply with the requirements of the Environmental Quality (Scheduled Wastes) Regulations 2005 (EQSWR 2005). Contents: Introduction To Environmental Quality Act 1974 (Act 127), Environmental Quality (Scheduled Wastes) Regulations, 2005, Management and Handling of Scheduled Wastes. Benefits to Clients and Participants to Undertake this Training: Support waste generators to comply with Regulation 15 of EQSWR 2005; provide awareness and better understanding on the requirement of EQSWR 2005; guide participants to comply with required documentations; assist in practical handling of scheduled wastes; disseminate information on best waste management practices (wastes to wealth) via waste elimination, reduction, reutilization, recycling, recovery, treatment and disposal.

course_name: Workshop on The Preparation of Scheduled Waste Information (Waste Card)
description: This workshop is designed to provide a step-by-step process to the participants to prepare good quality SW information (waste card) for the SW generated by using a SW SAMPLE TEMPLATE provided. The workshop will also guide participants with laboratory practical exercises and attempt to provide the most appropriate safety information of the SW for the waste card. The completed waste card could be used by the transporter as safety and emergency precautions or as instructions during the transportation or by the handler during storage, of the SW. At the end of the workshop, it is expected that participants should be able to develop and prepare the SW card themselves. Workshop Objectives: Enhance knowledge on SW information requirements; guide appropriate approach to develop/prepare SW information; guide step-by-step review of SDS information; expose participants to lab practical exercises testing physical properties of SW.

The importance of audit is further stressed in Clause 3.1 whereby management of the company shall ensure the availability of competent personnel to ensure effective implementation of halal internal control system. Halal Internal audit is one of the important tools which provide proof indicating halal internal control system within the organization is implemented and in compliance with regulatory, standard and system requirements. This training course introduces participants on the concept, process flow and activities involves during the internal audit.

description: Food and drinks are one of the fast moving products not just in Malaysia but all around the world, as food and drinks are essential needs for man to survive. Demand for food and drinks increases parallel with the world population. Globally the demand for food and drinks defer based on location, education and religion practice by man. Islam being the second largest religion in the world also has set specific requirements for food and drinks which is known as halal food and drinks. Muslim must abide to these requirements as a sign of devotion to Allah s.w.t (God). This training program is aimed to furnish the participants both Muslim and Non- Muslim to understand the requirements in producing halal food and drinks. The focus in on Halal Food Supply Chain which starts from the source of raw materials to the consumption of halal food and drinks, as always been highlighted as `From Farm to Fork’. This course will enrich participants with exposure and elevate participant knowledge in defining and translating the general requirement to required practices in the production area. This practice is also known as Halal Best Practices will ensure the premise to comply with the basic requirements for Malaysia Halal Certification.

address: No. 1, Jalan SS2/67, 47300 Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia
description: Man needs food to survive, unfortunately food can become the source of illness if not being prepared in a hygiene manner. In Malaysia, food poisoning cases was mainly caused by insanitary food handling procedures. The rate of unhygienic premises decreases in 20131 due to increase in monitoring program conducted by Ministry of Health. Malaysia has been selected as the number one destination for Muslim tourist, therefore it is important to ensure the food, we Malaysians serve to our visitors are of good quality and safe for consumption. Quality and safe foods are prepared by food handlers who implement Good Hygiene Practices and Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) or Good Catering Practices (GCP). GMP or GCP are procedures which dictates food and drinks to be prepared in an environment which is clean and hygienic, using quality and safe ingredients and ensuring no contamination occurs during processing of food and drinks. The objectives of the program: To introduce the type food contaminants and factors effecting food and drink. To enhance knowledge on food safety system requirements such as premise, process control, material control and others To relate food safety elements to the daily practices in food processing area To highlights the importance of crucial practices such segregation of equipment and utensils, temperature control, proper storage and personnel hygiene.

course_name: Webinar 24 June 2026 – The Acceptance Gap: Why We Know Better but Don’t Do Better
description: We live in a world overflowing with knowledge, frameworks, and best practices. Most people already know what “good” looks like — communicate clearly, give feedback early, collaborate across teams, take ownership, speak up, follow through. And yet, in the moments that matter, behaviour often doesn’t match intention. This stubborn space between knowing and doing is the Acceptance Gap. In many workplaces, the challenge isn’t a lack of awareness or skill. It’s the quiet, internal resistance that shows up as hesitation, avoidance, overthinking, or waiting for someone else to move first. People are trained, briefed, and fully prepared — but still unwilling to act. Not because they don’t care, but because something deeper is at play.

description: The best leaders are not necessarily those who have all the answers, but who ask the right questions. In this engaging program, discover what matters most to you as a leader. By clarifying your north star, you can first lead yourself, and then, others. It’s an incredibly challenging time to be a leader. AI disruption, shifting geopolitics, rapid technological change, and global uncertainties have turned agility and resilience into from buzzwords to daily essentials. Many leaders promoted to powerful positions have discovered that such positions do not themselves guarantee success or impact. They still face the challenge of having to “get things done” on a daily basis. If as a leader, you would no doubt have discovered that leadership is more than about power and authority. Neither a big title nor a “powerful” position makes a person an effective leader. Ultimately, it is about the leader’s values and the leader’s ability to act on them that defines his or her leadership agency, and is the true measure of success. As companies pivot, restructure, and think about building resilience for the future, it’s more important than ever for leaders to learn how to harness their leadership potential to solve pressing challenges. This program will equip you to do just that.

description: Program Overview Master impactful presentations in this dynamic two-day workshop. Day 1 builds your core foundation, focusing on audience analysis (WIIFM) and structuring clear messages using the STAR method. We will immediately integrate generative AI tools like Gemini and Chat GPT to accelerate script development, write effective titles, and design attention-grabbing openings. You will also refine essential verbal and non-verbal communication skills, learning how your body language and tone impact your message. Day 2 advances these skills into practical application. You will learn to pitch products by defining Unique Selling Points (USPs) and Unique Perceived Benefits (UPBs), using AI to enhance your brainstorming. We then focus on managing nervousness and maximizing PowerPoint, leveraging AI to improve visual design. The program culminates in live, interactive presentation exercises, where you will apply these new techniques and receive comprehensive feedback.

This 2-day training program is designed to equip marketing and sales professionals with hands-on skills to integrate AI into their daily workflow. From creating powerful ad campaigns to automating lead nurturing and optimizing content for SEO, participants will learn how to build an intelligent, data-driven digital strategy using the latest AI-powered tools. This course focuses on practical applications with tools like ChatGPT, Canva AI, Jasper, HubSpot, Surfer SEO, and more—empowering participants to work smarter, not harder.

description: PROGRAMME OVERVIEW When we say the word, “communication,” what do you think of? Many people will think of the spoken word. People who are hearing impaired, however, might think of sign language. People who are visually impaired might think of Braille as well as sounds. In this workshop, participants will be able to explore the different ways in which we communicate. The goal of the workshop is to help participants understand the impact of different communication techniques, the effect they have on other people, and how improving the way you communicate can make a huge difference in the workplace and at home.

fee: RM1,600.00 nett per person (inclusive of 8% service tax)
description: INTRODUCTION When we use the word 'negotiation', many people think of big organisation or business discussions or secret meeting with Union representatives. This is a misconception as we negotiate all the time in our normal daily life. We all at some point have discussed with a few friends where to eat or drink, or something as simple as who is going to take the rubbish out. Not all our negotiations are trivial. Discussions with your manager regarding salary or the price you pay for a new home or car, are all situations that involve negotiating! During this training, participants will get an understanding of the phases of negotiation, tools to use during negotiation, and ways to build win-win solutions.

fee: RM1,600.00 nett per person (inclusive of 8% service tax)
description: Introduction. Complexity leadership is an understanding of how successful organizations thrive in turbulent times. It doesn’t focus on the leader as the only driving force for success. Instead, it puts an emphasis on the whole system of an organization, including its social interactions and ways to manage complexity. Complexity leadership focuses on emergent processes within complex systems and suggests that leadership needs to operate at all levels in a process-oriented, contextual, and interactive fashion. The model emphasizes the importance of social interactions within organizations and illustrates the key role of the leader in enabling change.
